vulnerability.title: Ensure All User Initialization Files Have Mode 0740 Or Less Permissive
vulnerability.description: Set the mode of the user initialization files, including the root user, to 0740 with the following commands: $ sudo chmod 0740 /root/.INIT_FILE $ sudo chmod 0740 /home/USER/.INIT_FILE
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: var_user_initialization_files_regex='' readarray -t interactive_users < <(awk -F: '$3==0 || $3>=1000 {print $1}' /etc/passwd) readarray -t interactive_users_home < <(awk -F: '$3==0 || $3>=1000 {print $6}' /etc/passwd) readarray -t interactive_users_shell < <(awk -F: '$3==0 || $3>=1000 {print $7}' /etc/passwd) USERS_IGNORED_REGEX='nobody|nfsnobody' for (( i=0; i<"${#interactive_users[@]}"; i++ )); do if ! grep -qP "$USERS_IGNORED_REGEX" <<< "${interactive_users[$i]}" && \ [ "${interactive_users_shell[$i]}" != "/sbin/nologin" ]; then readarray -t init_files < <(find "${interactive_users_home[$i]}" -maxdepth 1 \ -exec basename {} \; | grep -P "$var_user_initialization_files_regex") for file in "${init_files[@]}"; do chmod u-s,g-wxs,o= "${interactive_users_home[$i]}/$file" done fi done

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes file permissions on user initialization files to a more restrictive mode. This is a standard STIG hardening step, does not modify authentication mechanisms, bootloader, or critical services, and is unlikely to cause lockout or system instability.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; User scripts that expect more permissive permissions may fail; Incorrectly identifying user home directories could affect non‑interactive accounts

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for openscap_023 (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_file_permission_user_init_files_root). Could not verify.

review_feedback: Review rejected the fix Suggestions: Use a single find command with -path and -perm to set mode 0740 recursively on matching files.; Quote variables and use proper glob expansion or arrays to avoid word splitting.; Add error handling to ensure chmod only runs on files where it succeeds.; Consider using chmod -R 0740 on the home directories after confirming file type.; Validate that only regular files are targeted and skip symlinks or directories.

concerns: Chmod commands failed with exit code 255 due to improper handling of wildcards and quoting.
The script attempts to apply chmod to many file patterns manually instead of using a robust find/exec approach.
Potential race condition when modifying files owned by other users.
May not correctly handle all shell initialization files if regex is empty or misconfigured.
suggested_improvements: Use a single find command with -path and -perm to set mode 0740 recursively on matching files.
Quote variables and use proper glob expansion or arrays to avoid word splitting.
Add error handling to ensure chmod only runs on files where it succeeds.
Consider using chmod -R 0740 on the home directories after confirming file type.
Validate that only regular files are targeted and skip symlinks or directories.
